orgs.Primary Focus: Progressive local/state electoral organization — identity unconfirmed
orgs.Evidence Summary: VERIFY. No nationally prominent org by this name found.

orgs.Primary Focus: Georgia-based voting rights and voter mobilization organization
orgs.Evidence Summary: Founded by Stacey Abrams. VERIFY current status and 2026 activity — relationship to ProGeorgia ecosystem unclear post-NGP closure.

orgs.Primary Focus: Climate-first voter mobilization for Americans 60+ in battleground states
orgs.States of Operation: AZ, PA, MI, NH, WI, GA
orgs.Evidence Summary: Founded by Bill McKibben. 100,000+ volunteers self-reported. Distinctive senior climate niche with no direct competitor. Multiple independent sources.
orgs.One-Sentence Pitch: Bill McKibben's climate mobilization org for Americans 60+ — 100,000+ volunteers in AZ, PA, MI, NH, WI, and GA, targeting the demographic that turns out most reliably and has the most to lose from inaction on climate.
orgs.2026 Priorities: Organizing 100,000+ senior volunteers around two interlocking 2026 campaigns: Make Polluters Pay (pushing state climate superfund legislation in MA, NJ, MD, CT, and others) and democracy protection (protests, civic engagement, and electoral mobilization in AZ, PA, MI, NH, WI, and GA ahead of November).

orgs.Primary Focus: National grassroots network for civic engagement, GOTV, and candidate endorsements
orgs.Evidence Summary: Historically general-election focused with strong track record. 2026 primary engagement is new and expanded — monitor.
orgs.One-Sentence Pitch: The 5,000-chapter grassroots network that has been the backbone of Democratic civic engagement since 2017, now expanding into contested primaries in 2026 for the first time — backing progressive challengers in MN-Sen, NY-10, and others.
orgs.2026 Priorities: Running the largest primary program in the organization's history in 2026 — endorsing and deploying grassroots network resources to progressive challengers in Democratic primaries (Flanagan MN-Sen, Lander NY-10, Clark GA-13, and others) while simultaneously mobilizing 5,000+ local chapters to block the MAGA agenda and drive midterm turnout in swing districts.

orgs.Primary Focus: Clearinghouse directing donor funds to local grassroots orgs in battleground states nationally
orgs.Evidence Summary: Raised $6.2M in 2025. Rigorous vetting process. High-leverage model. Multiple practitioner endorsements. 2026 focus: Battleground Alliance for House flip.
orgs.One-Sentence Pitch: The most rigorously vetted funding intermediary in the progressive ecosystem — a single grant reaches 145 vetted grassroots orgs across 29 states, with a 2026 Battleground Alliance focus on the House flip map.
orgs.2026 Priorities: Funding the Battleground Alliance — a coordinated coalition of local and national organizing groups in every top House flip district — through a two-phase 2026 strategy: accountability organizing through June to hold vulnerable Republicans on the record, then voter mobilization July through November to translate that groundwork into a Democratic House majority.

orgs.Primary Focus: Rural and small-town multiracial organizing and voter engagement in NC
orgs.Evidence Summary: People's Action affiliate. Distinctive rural white working-class focus fills gap other orgs don't. Essential for Cooper Senate path.
orgs.One-Sentence Pitch: The only organization doing sustained rural multiracial organizing in North Carolina's small towns — reaching white working-class voters alongside communities of color in precisely the geography Roy Cooper needs to flip the Senate seat.
orgs.2026 Priorities: Organizing rural and small-town working-class communities across North Carolina for the 2026 cycle — turning out the white working-class and rural multiracial voters that Roy Cooper's Senate campaign needs to flip a seat Democrats haven't held since 2008, in precisely the counties where statewide Democrats have historically bled votes.

orgs.Tax Status: 501(c)(3) via LCV Education Fund / 501(c)(4) via LCV — not independently incorporated
orgs.Primary Focus: LCV affiliate — climate + Latino voter focus in AZ
orgs.Evidence Summary: Battleground Alliance AZ partner + MVP blog (103K doors in 2024). LCV affiliate adds credibility.
orgs.One-Sentence Pitch: The only organization in Arizona running climate organizing specifically through Latino communities — 103K doors knocked in 2024, backed by LCV and the Battleground Alliance, in the exact geography that decides Arizona statewide races.
orgs.Fiscal Sponsor: LCV Education Fund (c3 activity) / League of Conservation Voters (c4 activity)

orgs.Founded: Must get the right statewide group in NC and they do have a c3
orgs.Sub-type: Black labor civic engagement (tax-deductible giving vehicle for APRI)
orgs.Primary Focus: Serves as the 501(c)(3) tax-deductible giving vehicle for the APRI ecosystem; receives contributions and channels them to support APRI's civic engagement, voter registration, and GOTV programs through the national chapter network.
orgs.Issue / Constituency Tags: Black voters, Labor, Voting rights, Civic engagement
orgs.States of Operation: National (chapters in 33 states: AL, AR, AZ, CA, CO, DC, DE, FL, GA, IL, IN, KY, LA, MA, MD, ME, MI, MN, MO, MS, NC, NJ, NV, NY, OH, OK, PA, SC, TN, TX, VA, WA, WI)
orgs.Evidence Summary: Companion 501(c)(3) to APRI (ORG-102); shares president, address, and mission; 990s consistently filed since 1980; board includes Eleanor Holmes Norton, Dr. William Julius Wilson, and Ernest Green — strong governance credibility. Donor-facing rationale is tax deductibility, not independent program capacity.
orgs.One-Sentence Pitch: The tax-deductible giving vehicle for America's oldest Black labor-civil rights civic engagement network — the right entry point for donors who need a c3 to fund APRI's 114-chapter GOTV operation.
orgs.Key Risks & Flags: (1) APREF has no independent program capacity — it is a conduit for tax-deductible giving to APRI; donors are effectively funding APRI through this vehicle. (2) Revenue is small and election-cycle-dependent; FY2025 $144K suggests off-cycle thinning. (3) All risks flagged in ORG-102 (APRI) apply here — the two entities are operationally inseparable. (4) Donors should understand they are not funding a standalone c3 organization with its own staff and programs.

orgs.Primary Focus: Year-round state and local election administration advocacy and voter access expansion
orgs.States of Operation: AZ, FL, GA, MI, NV, OH, PA, WI
orgs.Evidence Summary: Expanded access for 13M+ voters in 2024 cycle. Multiple independent sources. Fills gap other orgs miss.
orgs.One-Sentence Pitch: The year-round watchdog on election administration in eight battleground states — expanded ballot access for 13M+ voters in 2024 by fighting the administrative suppression attempts that fly under most donors' radar.
orgs.2026 Priorities: Defending voter access in eight battleground states (AZ, FL, GA, MI, NV, OH, PA, WI) against a wave of 187+ restrictive voting bills moving through state legislatures in 2026 — focused on fighting proof-of-citizenship mandates, mail ballot restrictions, and voter purges that disproportionately affect communities of color.

orgs.Primary Focus: Electoral endorsements and fusion ballot line supporting progressive candidates nationally
orgs.States of Operation: NY, CT, NJ, PA, OR, MD, DC, WI, RI, CA, WV
orgs.Evidence Summary: 25+ year track record. 2026: endorsing primary challengers. Does NOT challenge Frontline members. Donor alert: may conflict with DCCC-aligned strategy.
orgs.One-Sentence Pitch: Twenty-five years of progressive electoral infrastructure — fusion ballot lines, primary endorsements, and a 2026 commitment to challenge incumbent Democrats on the progressive wing while leaving Frontline members alone.
orgs.2026 Priorities: Endorsing and operationally supporting progressive primary challengers up and down the ballot in 2026 — with active slates in NC, OH, CO, OR, GA, and DC — while explicitly declining to challenge DCCC Frontline incumbents, threading the needle between movement accountability and general election pragmatism.

orgs.Primary Focus: Statewide voter registration, GOTV, power building for communities of color in VA
orgs.Evidence Summary: Multi-cycle track record in VA. Credited with helping flip VA legislature. Multiple independent sources.
orgs.One-Sentence Pitch: Virginia's anchor BIPOC voter mobilization organization — credited with helping flip the state legislature, and currently running turnout programs for two competitive 2026 House races.
orgs.2026 Priorities: Following the successful April 2026 redistricting referendum, pivoting to BIPOC voter mobilization for the November House races in the redrawn Virginia congressional map — with particular focus on the competitive districts created by the new lines and ongoing voter registration in communities of color statewide.
